political.s: FEC: $17K PAC donations; 38% to Republican, 62% to Democratic committees.

charity.s: $35M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.02% of revenue) via Walgreen Co. Charitable Trust.
charity.amt: Tens of millions annually (in-kind + monetary, not fully disclosed)
charity.focus: Health access, child nutrition, disaster relief, community giving

environment.s: CDP Climate Change: B (2024) — management-band disclosure; discloses emissions data but limited target ambition.

labor.s: Mental Health at Work Pledge signatory (since 2023): public commitment to gold-standard workplace mental-health policy, C-suite accountability, and measurable outcomes.

dei.s: HRC Corporate Equality Index: 100/100 — Equality 100 Leader (2025). Top-rated LGBTQ+ workplace policies, benefits, and culture. HRC Corporate Equality Index 100/100 (2025) — top-tier LGBTQ+ workplace equality rating.

guns.s: No federal firearms license on record for this company in the ATF FFL registry — no licensed firearms dealing or manufacturing.

privacy.s: 2 HHS OCR HIPAA breaches reported; 147K individuals affected total. Most recent: 2020-02-04. Indiana jury awarded $1.44M after Walgreens pharmacist disclosed prescription records.

wiki.extract: Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc. (WBA) was an American multinational holding company headquartered in Deerfield, Illinois. The company was formed on December 31, 2014, after Walgreens bought the 55% stake in Alliance Boots that it did not already own. The total price of the acquisition was $4.9 billion in cash and 144.3 million common shares with fair value of $10.7 billion. Walgreens had previously purchased 45% of the company for $4.0 billion and 83.4 million common shares in August 2012 with an option to purchase the remaining shares within three years. Walgreens became a subsidiary of the ne

sampleRecalls.title: Walgreens Recalls Disney Baby Winnie the Pooh Rattle Sets Due to Choking Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves the Disney Baby Winnie the Pooh Rattle Set. The set includes three rattles and is marked for ages 3 months and above. The rattle, which has the character Winnie the Pooh as part of the toy, is blue and red with green and yellow shapes and is approximately 6.7 inches high
hazards: The feet on the Winnie the Pooh rattle can detach, posing a choking hazard to young children.
injuries: Walgreens has received 8 reports of the feet on the Winnie the Pooh rattle detaching. No Injuries have been reported.
products: Disney Baby Winnie the Pooh Rattle Sets

sampleRecords.event_description: Injured by object held or wielded by person
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was using a photo cutting trimmer board to trim photos and amputated their left thumb tip without bone loss.

sampleRecords.part_of_body: Exterior and musculoskeletal structures of the back  unspecified
sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was working inside the pharmacy unit pick-up window. The employee exited the pharmacy unit and was walking through the lobby when they slipped on the wet floor and landed on their back. The employee was hospitalized with a fractured spine.

sampleRecords.employer: Walgreen Co. dba Walgreens Distribution Center
sampleRecords.final_narrative: On June 29, 2025, at approximately 10:25 PM, a warehouse employee was moving product from one pallet to another. As she was turning, shrink wrap got caught on her leg, and she fell on the ground. The employee sustained fractures to her left arm, requiring hospitalization and surgery.

sampleRecords.nature: Multiple severe wounds and internal injuries
sampleRecords.final_narrative: Employees collided with each other behind a photo counter. One of them fell into a poster-cutting machine, then to the floor. The employee suffered three broken ribs and a spleen injury and was hospitalized for surgery.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee had been working behind the front register. The employee was walking when they fell over a basket and sustained a broken hip. The employee was hospitalized.
